message.content:
Thoroughly explore this maimai-assistant project. I need to understand:

1. The full project structure (all files)
2. How search works (src/modules/search.py) - the search flow, JS extraction, pagination, error handling
3. How messaging works (src/modules/message.py) - template rendering, batch sending, confirmation flow
4. Anti-detection mechanisms (src/core/anti_detection.py) - what stealth measures are in place
5. Browser management (src/core/browser.py) - how the browser is managed
6. Rate limiting (src/utils/rate_limiter.py) - current limits and implementation
7. The CLI pipeline command (src/cli/main.py) - how search+message pipeline works
8. Delay utilities (src/utils/delays.py) - human-like delay implementation
9. Retry logic (src/utils/retry.py)
10. Session management (src/core/session.py)
11. Auth flow (src/modules/auth.py)
12. Selector discovery (src/modules/selector_discovery.py)

Read ALL these files completely. I want to identify weaknesses, missing features, and improvement opportunities for automated search and messaging on Maimai platform.

   466→                    parent = await link.evaluate_handle('el => el.closest("div, li, article") || el.parentElement.parentElement')
   467→
   468→                    title = ""
   469→                    company = ""
   470→
   471→                    if parent:
   472→                        # Get all text content and try to parse
   473→                        try:
   474→                            full_text = await parent.inner_text()
   475→                            lines = [l.strip() for l in full_text.split('\n') if l.strip()]
   476→
   477→                            # Usually: name, then title/company info
   478→                            for line in lines:
   479→                                if line == name:
   480→                                    continue
   481→                                if '好友' in line or '关注' in line:
   482→                                    continue
   483→                                if not title and len(line) < 50:
   484→                                    title = line
   485→                                elif not company and len(line) < 50:
   486→                                    company = line
   487→                                    break
   488→                        except Exception:
   489→                            pass
   490→
   491→                    profile_url = href if href.startswith('http') else f"https://maimai.cn{href}"
   492→
   493→                    candidates.append(CandidateBasic(
   494→                        maimai_id=maimai_id,
   495→                        name=name,
   496→                        title=title,
   497→                        company=company,
   498→                        profile_url=profile_url
   499→                    ))
   500→
   501→                except Exception as e:
   502→                    logger.debug(f"Failed to extract from link: {e}")
   503→                    continue
   504→
   505→        # Strategy 2: If no links found, try button-based detection
   506→        if not candidates:
   507→            logger.debug("Trying button-based detection")
   508→            buttons = await page.query_selector_all('button:has-text("好友"), button:has-text("关注")')
   509→
   510→            for button in buttons:
   511→                try:
   512→                    parent = await button.evaluate_handle('el => el.closest("div, li, article")')
   513→                    if not parent:
   514→                        continue
   515→
   516→                    # Find link in parent
   517→                    link = await parent.query_selector('a[href*="/contact"], a[href*="/u/"]')
   518→                    if link:
   519→                        href = await link.get_attribute('href')
   520→                        name = await link.inner_text()
   521→
   522→                        maimai_id = None
   523→                        if '/contact/detail/' in href:
   524→                            maimai_id = href.split('/contact/detail/')[-1].split('?')[0]
   525→                        elif '/u/' in href:
   526→                            maimai_id = href.split('/u/')[-1].split('?')[0]
   527→
   528→                        if maimai_id and name:
   529→                            candidates.append(CandidateBasic(

   531→                                name=name.strip(),
   532→                                title="",
   533→                                company="",
   534→                                profile_url=href
   535→                            ))
   536→                except Exception:
   537→                    continue
   538→
   539→        # Filter out premium placeholder profiles
   540→        before_filter = len(candidates)
   541→        candidates = [c for c in candidates if c.name not in PREMIUM_PLACEHOLDER_NAMES]
   542→        filtered_count = before_filter - len(candidates)

   570→                if not existing:

   574→
   575→                if existing:

   228→async def send_message_batch(
   229→    candidates: List[dict],
   230→    template_name: str,
   231→    common_variables: Dict[str, str],
   232→    browser: BrowserManager,
   233→    skip_messaged: bool = True,
   234→    interactive: bool = False,
   235→) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   236→    """
   237→    Batch send messages to a list of candidates.
   238→
   239→    Flow:
   240→    1. Filter already-messaged candidates
   241→    2. Auto-fill name/company/position variables per candidate
   242→    3. Render message template
   243→    4. Show candidate list + first message preview
   244→    5. Single confirmation
   245→    6. Send one by one, checking rate limit each time
   246→    7. Skip failures and record
   247→    8. Return result stats
   248→
   249→    Args:
   250→        candidates: List of dicts with maimai_id, name, company, title
   251→        template_name: Name of message template
   252→        common_variables: Shared variables (recruiter_name, target_position, etc.)
   253→        browser: Browser manager instance
   254→        skip_messaged: Skip candidates already messaged (default True)
   255→        interactive: Enable interactive selector discovery on first failure
   256→
   257→    Returns:
   258→        Dict with total, sent, failed, skipped, rate_limited, details
   259→    """
   260→    config = get_config()
   261→    rate_limiter = get_rate_limiter()
   262→    db_manager = get_db_manager()
   263→
   264→    # Get template
   265→    try:
   266→        template = config.get_template(template_name)
   267→    except Exception:
   268→        raise ValidationError(f"Template not found: {template_name}")
   269→
   270→    # Create shared discovered selectors for interactive mode
   271→    if interactive:
   272→        from src.modules.selector_discovery import DiscoveredSelectors
   273→        discovered_selectors = DiscoveredSelectors()
   274→    else:
   275→        discovered_selectors = None
   276→
   277→    results = {
   278→        'total': len(candidates),
   279→        'sent': 0,
   280→        'failed': 0,
   281→        'skipped': 0,
   282→        'rate_limited': 0,
   283→        'details': [],
   284→    }
   285→
   286→    # Filter already-messaged candidates
   287→    send_list = []
   288→    for c in candidates:
   289→        if skip_messaged:
   290→            with db_manager.session_scope() as session:
   291→                existing_msg = session.query(Message).filter_by(
   292→                    candidate_maimai_id=c['maimai_id'],
   293→                    status='sent'
   294→                ).first()
   295→                if existing_msg:
   296→                    results['skipped'] += 1
   297→                    results['details'].append({
   298→                        'maimai_id': c['maimai_id'],
   299→                        'name': c['name'],
   300→                        'status': 'skipped',
   301→                        'reason': 'already messaged',
   302→                    })

   304→        send_list.append(c)

   306→    if not send_list:

   309→
   310→    # Build preview for first candidate

   313→    first_content = render_template(template['content'], first_vars)
   314→
   315→    # Display summary panel
   316→    from rich.table import Table
   317→
   318→    summary_text = Text()

   320→    summary_text.append(f"{template_name}\n")
   321→    summary_text.append("Candidates: ", style="bold")

   323→    summary_text.append("Will send to: ", style="bold")
   324→    summary_text.append(f"{len(send_list)} candidates\n\n")
   325→    summary_text.append("Message Preview ", style="bold cyan")
   326→    summary_text.append(f"(first candidate):\n", style="dim")
   327→    summary_text.append("To: ", style="bold cyan")
   328→    recipient = first['name']
   329→    if first.get('title') and first.get('company'):
   330→        recipient += f" ({first['title']} @ {first['company']})"
   331→    summary_text.append(f"{recipient}\n\n")
   332→    summary_text.append(first_content)
   333→
   334→    console.print(Panel(
   335→        summary_text,
   336→        title="Pipeline Summary",
   337→        border_style="green",
   338→        padding=(1, 2)
   339→    ))
   340→
   341→    # Single confirmation (run in executor to avoid nested event loop)
   342→    loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
   343→    confirm = await loop.run_in_executor(
   344→        None,
   345→        lambda: questionary.confirm(
   346→            f"Confirm batch send to {len(send_list)} candidates?"
   347→        ).unsafe_ask()
   348→    )
   349→
   350→    if not confirm:
   351→        console.print("[yellow]Batch send cancelled.[/yellow]")
   352→        return results
   353→
   354→    # Send messages one by one
   355→    from rich.progress import Progress, BarColumn, TextColumn as ProgressTextColumn, MofNCompleteColumn
   356→
   357→    with Progress(
   358→        ProgressTextColumn("[progress.description]{task.description}"),
   359→        BarColumn(),

   367→            try:

   380→
   381→            # Build per-candidate variables

   383→                **common_variables,
   384→                'name': c['name'],
   385→                'company': c['company'],

   387→            }
   388→            content = render_template(template['content'], variables)

   390→            try:

   393→                    template_name=template_name,
   394→                    variables=variables,
   395→                    browser=browser,
   396→                    skip_confirmation=True,
   397→                    interactive=interactive,
   398→                    discovered_selectors=discovered_selectors,
   399→                    _skip_rate_check=True,
   400→                )
   401→
   402→                if success:
   403→                    results['sent'] += 1
   404→                    results['details'].append({
   405→                        'maimai_id': c['maimai_id'],
   406→                        'name': c['name'],
   407→                        'status': 'sent',
   408→                    })
   409→                else:
   410→                    results['failed'] += 1
   411→                    results['details'].append({
   412→                        'maimai_id': c['maimai_id'],
   413→                        'name': c['name'],
   414→                        'status': 'failed',
   415→                    })
   416→            except RateLimitExceededError as e:
   417→                remaining = len(send_list) - (results['sent'] + results['failed'])
   418→                results['rate_limited'] = remaining
   419→                results['details'].append({
   420→                    'maimai_id': c['maimai_id'],
   421→                    'name': c['name'],
   422→                    'status': 'rate_limited',
   423→                    'reason': str(e),
   424→                })
   425→                console.print(f"\n[yellow]Rate limit reached: {e}[/yellow]")
   426→                break
   427→            except Exception as e:
   428→                results['failed'] += 1
   429→                results['details'].append({
   430→                    'maimai_id': c['maimai_id'],
   431→                    'name': c['name'],
   432→                    'status': 'failed',
   433→                    'reason': str(e),
   434→                })
   435→                logger.error(f"Failed to send message to {c['name']}: {e}")
   436→

    82→async def random_mouse_move(page: Page, target_x: int, target_y: int) -> None:
    83→    """
    84→    Move mouse to target position with human-like random trajectory.
    85→
    86→    Uses bezier curve-like movement with random control points.
    87→
    88→    Args:
    89→        page: Playwright page instance
    90→        target_x: Target X coordinate
    91→        target_y: Target Y coordinate
    92→    """
    93→    # Get current mouse position (approximate from viewport center if unknown)
    94→    viewport = page.viewport_size
    95→    if viewport:
    96→        current_x = viewport['width'] // 2
    97→        current_y = viewport['height'] // 2
    98→    else:
    99→        current_x, current_y = 500, 400
   100→
   101→    # Generate intermediate points for natural movement
   102→    steps = random.randint(10, 25)
   103→
   104→    for i in range(steps):
   105→        # Calculate progress with easing
   106→        progress = (i + 1) / steps
   107→        eased_progress = ease_out_quad(progress)
   108→
   109→        # Add slight randomness to path
   110→        jitter_x = random.randint(-3, 3)
   111→        jitter_y = random.randint(-3, 3)
   112→
   113→        # Interpolate position
   114→        x = int(current_x + (target_x - current_x) * eased_progress + jitter_x)
   115→        y = int(current_y + (target_y - current_y) * eased_progress + jitter_y)
   116→
   117→        await page.mouse.move(x, y)
   118→
   119→        # Random micro-delay between movements
   120→        delay = random.uniform(0.005, 0.02)
   121→        await page.wait_for_timeout(delay * 1000)
   122→
   123→    # Final move to exact target
   124→    await page.mouse.move(target_x, target_y)
   125→
   126→
   127→def ease_out_quad(t: float) -> float:
   128→    """Quadratic ease-out function for natural movement."""
   129→    return 1 - (1 - t) ** 2
   130→
   131→
   132→async def human_scroll(page: Page, direction: str = 'down', distance: int = None) -> None:
   133→    """
   134→    Perform human-like scrolling with variable speed.
   135→
   136→    Args:
   137→        page: Playwright page instance
   138→        direction: 'up' or 'down'
   139→        distance: Scroll distance in pixels (random if None)
   140→    """
   141→    if distance is None:
   142→        distance = random.randint(200, 600)
   143→
   144→    if direction == 'up':
   145→        distance = -distance
   146→
   147→    # Break scroll into smaller chunks
   148→    chunks = random.randint(3, 8)
   149→    chunk_size = distance // chunks
   150→
   151→    for i in range(chunks):
   152→        # Add variance to each chunk
   153→        actual_chunk = chunk_size + random.randint(-20, 20)
   154→
   155→        await page.mouse.wheel(0, actual_chunk)
   156→
   157→        # Variable delay between scroll chunks
   158→        delay = random.uniform(0.05, 0.15)

   160→
   161→    # Small pause after scrolling (human would look at content)

   165→async def human_click(page: Page, selector: str) -> None:
   166→    """

   169→    Moves mouse to element before clicking with slight offset.

   171→    Args:

   184→    # Calculate click position with slight random offset from center

   188→    offset_x = random.uniform(-box['width'] * 0.2, box['width'] * 0.2)

   190→
   191→    click_x = int(center_x + offset_x)

1→"""Browser manager for Playwright automation.
     2→
     3→Provides a singleton browser manager that handles browser lifecycle,
     4→session persistence, and anti-detection measures.
     5→"""
     6→
     7→import asyncio
     8→from pathlib import Path
     9→from typing import Optional
    10→from datetime import datetime
    11→
    12→from playwright.async_api import async_playwright, Browser, BrowserContext, Page, Playwright
    13→from loguru import logger
    14→
    15→from src.utils.config import get_config
    16→from src.core.anti_detection import apply_stealth_scripts, get_random_user_agent
    17→from src.core.session import SessionManager, get_session_manager
    18→from src.exceptions.errors import BrowserError, SessionExpiredError
    19→
    20→
    21→class BrowserManager:
    22→    """
    23→    Singleton browser manager for Maimai automation.
    24→
    25→    Handles browser lifecycle, session management, and provides
    26→    configured page instances with anti-detection measures.
    27→    """
    28→
    29→    _instance: Optional['BrowserManager'] = None
    30→    _lock: Optional[asyncio.Lock] = None
    31→
    32→    def __new__(cls):
    33→        if cls._instance is None:
    34→            cls._instance = super().__new__(cls)
    35→            cls._instance._initialized = False
    36→        return cls._instance
    37→
    38→    def __init__(self):
    39→        if self._initialized:
    40→            return
    41→
    42→        self._playwright: Optional[Playwright] = None
    43→        self._browser: Optional[Browser] = None
    44→        self._context: Optional[BrowserContext] = None
    45→        self._page: Optional[Page] = None
    46→        self._session_manager: SessionManager = get_session_manager()
    47→        self._config = get_config()
    48→        self._initialized = True
    49→
    50→    @property
    51→    def is_running(self) -> bool:
    52→        """Check if browser is currently running."""
    53→        return self._browser is not None and self._browser.is_connected()
    54→
    55→    @property
    56→    def page(self) -> Optional[Page]:
    57→        """Get current page instance."""
    58→        return self._page
    59→
    60→    @property
    61→    def context(self) -> Optional[BrowserContext]:
    62→        """Get current browser context."""
    63→        return self._context
    64→
    65→    @classmethod
    66→    def _ensure_lock(cls):
    67→        """Lazily create the asyncio.Lock inside a running event loop."""
    68→        if cls._lock is None:
    69→            cls._lock = asyncio.Lock()
    70→
    71→    async def start(self, headless: Optional[bool] = None) -> Page:
    72→        """
    73→        Start browser and return a configured page.
    74→
    75→        Args:
    76→            headless: Override headless setting from config
    77→
    78→        Returns:
    79→            Configured Playwright page instance
    80→
    81→        Raises:
    82→            BrowserError: If browser fails to start
    83→        """
    84→        self._ensure_lock()
    85→        async with self._lock:
    86→            if self.is_running:
    87→                logger.debug("Browser already running, returning existing page")
    88→                return self._page
    89→
    90→            try:
    91→                logger.info("Starting browser...")
    92→
    93→                # Start Playwright
    94→                self._playwright = await async_playwright().start()
    95→
    96→                # Get browser settings
    97→                if headless is None:
    98→                    headless = self._config.get('browser.headless', False)
    99→
   100→                timeout = self._config.get('browser.timeout', 30000)
   101→                viewport = self._config.get('browser.viewport', {'width': 1920, 'height': 1080})
   102→
   103→                # Browser launch arguments for anti-detection
   104→                launch_args = [

   113→
   114→                # Launch browser
   115→                self._browser = await self._playwright.chromium.launch(
   116→                    headless=headless,

   118→                )
   119→
   120→                # Check for saved session
   121→                storage_state_path = self._session_manager.get_storage_state_path()
   122→
   123→                # Create context with settings

   133→                    logger.info("Loading saved session")

   149→            except Exception as e:

1→"""Rate limiter for preventing account bans."""
     2→
     3→import asyncio
     4→from datetime import datetime, timedelta
     5→from typing import Tuple, Optional
     6→
     7→from loguru import logger
     8→from sqlalchemy import func
     9→
    10→from src.models.database import ActionLog, get_db_manager
    11→from src.utils.config import get_config
    12→from src.exceptions.errors import RateLimitExceededError
    13→
    14→
    15→class RateLimiter:
    16→    """Rate limiter for controlling action frequency."""
    17→
    18→    def __init__(self):
    19→        """Initialize rate limiter with configuration."""
    20→        self.config = get_config()
    21→        self.db_manager = get_db_manager()
    22→
    23→    def _get_limits(self, action_type: str) -> Tuple[int, int, int]:
    24→        """
    25→        Get rate limits for an action type.
    26→
    27→        Args:
    28→            action_type: Type of action ('search', 'view_profile', 'send_message')
    29→
    30→        Returns:
    31→            Tuple of (per_hour_limit, per_day_limit, min_interval_seconds)
    32→        """
    33→        action_map = {
    34→            'search': ('search_per_hour', 'search_per_day'),
    35→            'view_profile': ('profile_view_per_hour', 'profile_view_per_day'),
    36→            'send_message': ('message_per_hour', 'message_per_day'),
    37→        }
    38→
    39→        if action_type not in action_map:
    40→            raise ValueError(f"Unknown action type: {action_type}")
    41→
    42→        hour_key, day_key = action_map[action_type]
    43→
    44→        per_hour = self.config.get(f'rate_limits.{hour_key}', 20)
    45→        per_day = self.config.get(f'rate_limits.{day_key}', 100)
    46→        min_interval = self.config.get('rate_limits.min_interval_seconds', 30)
    47→
    48→        return per_hour, per_day, min_interval
    49→
    50→    def _count_actions(self, action_type: str, since: datetime) -> int:
    51→        """
    52→        Count actions of a type since a given time.
    53→
    54→        Args:
    55→            action_type: Type of action
    56→            since: Count actions since this time
    57→
    58→        Returns:
    59→            Number of actions
    60→        """
    61→        session = self.db_manager.get_session()
    62→        try:
    63→            count = session.query(func.count(ActionLog.id)).filter(
    64→                ActionLog.action_type == action_type,
    65→                ActionLog.timestamp >= since
    66→            ).scalar()
    67→
    68→            return count or 0
    69→
    70→        finally:
    71→            session.close()
    72→
    73→    def _get_last_action_time(self, action_type: Optional[str] = None) -> Optional[datetime]:
    74→        """
    75→        Get timestamp of last action.
    76→
    77→        Args:
    78→            action_type: Type of action, or None for any action
    79→
    80→        Returns:
    81→            Timestamp of last action, or None if no actions
    82→        """
    83→        session = self.db_manager.get_session()
    84→        try:
    85→            query = session.query(func.max(ActionLog.timestamp))
    86→
    87→            if action_type:
    88→                query = query.filter(ActionLog.action_type == action_type)
    89→
    90→            last_time = query.scalar()
    91→            return last_time
    92→
    93→        finally:
    94→            session.close()
    95→
    96→    def check_rate_limit(self, action_type: str) -> None:
    97→        """
    98→        Check if action is allowed under rate limits.
    99→
   100→        Args:
   101→            action_type: Type of action to check
   102→
   103→        Raises:
   104→            RateLimitExceededError: If rate limit is exceeded
   105→        """
   106→        per_hour, per_day, min_interval = self._get_limits(action_type)
   107→
   108→        now = datetime.utcnow()
   109→        one_hour_ago = now - timedelta(hours=1)
   110→        one_day_ago = now - timedelta(days=1)
   111→
   112→        # Check hourly limit
   113→        hourly_count = self._count_actions(action_type, one_hour_ago)
   114→        if hourly_count >= per_hour:
   115→            # Calculate wait time until oldest action expires
   116→            session = self.db_manager.get_session()
   117→            try:
   118→                oldest_in_window = session.query(ActionLog.timestamp).filter(
   119→                    ActionLog.action_type == action_type,
   120→                    ActionLog.timestamp >= one_hour_ago
   121→                ).order_by(ActionLog.timestamp.asc()).first()
   122→
   123→                if oldest_in_window:
   124→                    wait_until = oldest_in_window[0] + timedelta(hours=1)
   125→                    wait_seconds = int((wait_until - now).total_seconds())
   126→                else:
   127→                    wait_seconds = 3600
   128→
   129→            finally:
   130→                session.close()
   131→
   132→            raise RateLimitExceededError(
   133→                action_type=action_type,
   134→                wait_time=wait_seconds,
   135→                message=f"Hourly rate limit exceeded for {action_type}. "
   136→                        f"Limit: {per_hour}/hour. Wait {wait_seconds // 60} minutes."
   137→            )
   138→
   139→        # Check daily limit
   140→        daily_count = self._count_actions(action_type, one_day_ago)
   141→        if daily_count >= per_day:
   142→            session = self.db_manager.get_session()
   143→            try:
   144→                oldest_in_window = session.query(ActionLog.timestamp).filter(
   145→                    ActionLog.action_type == action_type,
   146→                    ActionLog.timestamp >= one_day_ago
   147→                ).order_by(ActionLog.timestamp.asc()).first()
   148→
   149→                if oldest_in_window:
   150→                    wait_until = oldest_in_window[0] + timedelta(days=1)
   151→                    wait_seconds = int((wait_until - now).total_seconds())
   152→                else:
   153→                    wait_seconds = 86400
   154→
   155→            finally:
   156→                session.close()
   157→
   158→            raise RateLimitExceededError(
   159→                action_type=action_type,
   160→                wait_time=wait_seconds,
   161→                message=f"Daily rate limit exceeded for {action_type}. "
   162→                        f"Limit: {per_day}/day. Wait {wait_seconds // 3600} hours."
   163→            )
   164→
   165→        # Check minimum interval
   166→        last_action = self._get_last_action_time()
   167→        if last_action:
   168→            elapsed = (now - last_action).total_seconds()
   169→            if elapsed < min_interval:
   170→                wait_seconds = int(min_interval - elapsed)
   171→                raise RateLimitExceededError(
   172→                    action_type='any',
   173→                    wait_time=wait_seconds,
   174→                    message=f"Minimum interval not met. Wait {wait_seconds} seconds."
   175→                )
   176→
   177→        logger.debug(f"Rate limit check passed for {action_type}")
   178→
   179→    def log_action(self, action_type: str, details: Optional[dict] = None) -> None:
   180→        """
   181→        Log an action to the database.
   182→
   183→        Args:
   184→            action_type: Type of action
   185→            details: Optional action details
   186→        """
   187→        session = self.db_manager.get_session()
   188→        try:
   189→            action_log = ActionLog(
   190→                action_type=action_type,
   191→                timestamp=datetime.utcnow(),
   192→                details=details or {}
   193→            )
   194→            session.add(action_log)
   195→            session.commit()
   196→
   197→            logger.debug(f"Logged action: {action_type}")
   198→
   199→        except Exception as e:
   200→            session.rollback()
   201→            logger.error(f"Failed to log action: {e}")
   202→            raise
   203→
   204→        finally:
   205→            session.close()
   206→
   207→    def get_quota_status(self, action_type: str) -> dict:
   208→        """
   209→        Get current quota status for an action type.
   210→
   211→        Args:
   212→            action_type: Type of action
   213→
   214→        Returns:
   215→            Dictionary with quota information
   216→        """
   217→        per_hour, per_day, _ = self._get_limits(action_type)
   218→
   219→        now = datetime.utcnow()
   220→        one_hour_ago = now - timedelta(hours=1)
   221→        one_day_ago = now - timedelta(days=1)
   222→
   223→        hourly_used = self._count_actions(action_type, one_hour_ago)
   224→        daily_used = self._count_actions(action_type, one_day_ago)
   225→
   226→        return {
   227→            'action_type': action_type,
   228→            'hourly': {
   229→                'used': hourly_used,
   230→                'limit': per_hour,
   231→                'remaining': max(0, per_hour - hourly_used)
   232→            },
   233→            'daily': {
   234→                'used': daily_used,
   235→                'limit': per_day,
   236→                'remaining': max(0, per_day - daily_used)
   237→            }
   238→        }
   239→
   240→
   241→async def async_check_rate_limit(action_type: str) -> None:
   242→    """
   243→    Check rate limit, waiting through any violations until the action is allowed.
   244→
   245→    Blocks until the rate limit clears — never raises RateLimitExceededError.
   246→
   247→    Args:
   248→        action_type: Type of action to check
   249→    """
   250→    rate_limiter = get_rate_limiter()
   251→    while True:
   252→        try:
   253→            rate_limiter.check_rate_limit(action_type)
   254→            return
   255→        except RateLimitExceededError as e:
   256→            wait = e.wait_time + 1  # +1s buffer
   257→            logger.info(f"Rate limit hit: {e}. Waiting {wait}s before retrying...")
   258→            await asyncio.sleep(wait)
   259→
   260→
   261→# Global rate limiter instance
   262→_rate_limiter = None
   263→
   264→
   265→def get_rate_limiter() -> RateLimiter:
   266→    """Get global rate limiter instance."""
   267→    global _rate_limiter
   268→    if _rate_limiter is None:
   269→        _rate_limiter = RateLimiter()
   270→    return _rate_limiter
   271→

   246→@cli.command()
   247→@click.option('--keywords', '-k', required=True, multiple=True, help='Search keywords (can specify multiple)')
   248→@click.option('--template', '-t', required=True, help='Message template name')
   249→@click.option('--var', '-v', multiple=True, help='Template variable (format: key=value)')
   250→@click.option('--company', '-c', multiple=True, help='Filter by company name (can specify multiple)')
   251→@click.option('--location', '-l', help='Filter by location')
   252→@click.option('--max-pages', '-p', type=int, default=1, help='Max pages to scrape per keyword')
   253→@click.option('--dry-run', is_flag=True, help='Search only, do not send messages')
   254→@click.option('--skip-messaged/--no-skip-messaged', default=True, help='Skip already messaged candidates')
   255→@click.option('--batch-pages', '-b', type=int, default=0, help='Send messages every N pages (0 = send after all pages)')
   256→@click.option('--interactive', '-i', is_flag=True, help='Enable interactive selector discovery when auto-detection fails')
   257→def pipeline(keywords, template, var, company, location, max_pages, dry_run, skip_messaged, batch_pages, interactive):
   258→    """
   259→    Search candidates and batch send messages in one command.
   260→
   261→    Combines multi-keyword search with batch messaging:
   262→    search → preview → confirm → send.
   263→
   264→    Use --batch-pages to interleave search and messaging every N pages.
   265→    Example: -p 10 --batch-pages 2 searches pages 1-2, sends messages,
   266→    then pages 3-4, sends messages, etc.
   267→
   268→    Examples:\n
   269→        maimai pipeline -k "前端工程师" -t greeting -v "recruiter_name=张三" -p 2\n
   270→        maimai pipeline -k "前端" -k "后端" -t greeting -v "recruiter_name=张三" --dry-run\n
   271→        maimai pipeline -k "前端" -t greeting -p 10 --batch-pages 2\n
   272→        maimai pipeline -k "前端" -t greeting -p 1 -i
   273→    """
   274→    from src.modules.message import send_message_batch
   275→    from src.core.browser import get_browser_manager
   276→
   277→    # Parse variables
   278→    variables = {}
   279→    for v in var:
   280→        if '=' in v:
   281→            key, value = v.split('=', 1)
   282→            variables[key.strip()] = value.strip()
   283→
   284→    async def _pipeline():
   285→        from src.modules.auth import ensure_logged_in
   286→
   287→        browser = get_browser_manager()
   288→        try:
   289→            # Ensure logged in BEFORE any search/message operations
   290→            await ensure_logged_in(browser)
   291→
   292→            if batch_pages > 0:
   293→                await _pipeline_batched(browser, keywords, company, location,
   294→                                        max_pages, batch_pages, template, variables,
   295→                                        dry_run, skip_messaged)
   296→            else:
   297→                await _pipeline_default(browser, keywords, company, location,
   298→                                        max_pages, template, variables,
   299→                                        dry_run, skip_messaged)
   300→        finally:
   301→            await browser.close()
   302→
   303→    async def _pipeline_default(browser, keywords, company, location,
   304→                                max_pages, template, variables, dry_run, skip_messaged):
   305→        """Original pipeline: search all pages, then send messages."""
   306→        all_candidates, seen_ids = await _search_all_keywords(
   307→            browser, keywords, company, location, max_pages
   308→        )
   309→
   310→        if not all_candidates:
   311→            console.print("[yellow]No candidates found for any keyword.[/yellow]")
   312→            return
   313→
   314→        _display_candidates_table(all_candidates, "Pipeline Results")
   315→
   316→        if dry_run:
   317→            console.print(f"\n[cyan][Dry Run] {len(all_candidates)} candidates found. No messages sent.[/cyan]")
   318→            return
   319→
   320→        results = await _send_batch(all_candidates, template, variables, browser, skip_messaged, interactive)
   321→        _display_send_results(results)
   322→
   323→    async def _pipeline_batched(browser, keywords, company, location,
   324→                                max_pages, batch_pages, template, variables,
   325→                                dry_run, skip_messaged):
   326→        """Batched pipeline: search N pages, send messages, repeat."""
   327→        from src.modules.auth import ensure_logged_in, check_login_status
   328→        from src.core.session import get_session_manager
   329→
   330→        seen_ids = set()
   331→        total_results = {'total': 0, 'sent': 0, 'failed': 0, 'skipped': 0, 'rate_limited': 0}
   332→        batch_num = 0
   333→        consecutive_empty_batches = 0
   334→
   335→        for batch_start in range(1, max_pages + 1, batch_pages):
   336→            batch_page_count = min(batch_pages, max_pages - batch_start + 1)
   337→            batch_num += 1
   338→            batch_end = batch_start + batch_page_count - 1
   339→
   340→            console.print(f"\n[bold magenta]━━━ Batch {batch_num}: Pages {batch_start}-{batch_end} ━━━[/bold magenta]")
   341→
   342→            batch_candidates, batch_seen = await _search_all_keywords(
   343→                browser, keywords, company, location, batch_page_count, start_page=batch_start
   344→            )
   345→            # Filter out globally seen IDs
   346→            batch_candidates = [c for c in batch_candidates if c.maimai_id not in seen_ids]
   347→            seen_ids.update(c.maimai_id for c in batch_candidates)
   348→
   349→            if not batch_candidates:
   350→                consecutive_empty_batches += 1
   351→                console.print(f"[yellow]No new candidates in batch {batch_num}.[/yellow]")
   352→
   353→                # If multiple consecutive batches return 0, session likely expired
   354→                if consecutive_empty_batches >= 2:
   355→                    console.print("[red]Multiple consecutive empty batches detected. Verifying login status...[/red]")
   356→                    if not await check_login_status(browser.page):
   357→                        console.print("[red]Session expired! Clearing session and re-authenticating...[/red]")
   358→                        session_manager = get_session_manager()
   359→                        await session_manager.clear()
   360→                        await ensure_logged_in(browser)
   361→                        consecutive_empty_batches = 0
   362→                        console.print("[green]Re-authenticated. Retrying current batch...[/green]")
   363→                        # Retry this batch by adjusting loop - rewind batch_start
   364→                        # We can't easily rewind the for loop, so just continue
   365→                        # and the next batch will use the new session
   366→                    else:
   367→                        console.print("[yellow]Session appears valid. Possibly no more results for these keywords.[/yellow]")
   368→                continue
   369→            else:
   370→                consecutive_empty_batches = 0
   371→
   372→            _display_candidates_table(batch_candidates, f"Batch {batch_num} Results")
   373→
   374→            if dry_run:
   375→                console.print(f"\n[cyan][Dry Run] Batch {batch_num}: {len(batch_candidates)} candidates found. No messages sent.[/cyan]")
   376→                continue
   377→
   378→            results = await _send_batch(batch_candidates, template, variables, browser, skip_messaged, interactive)
   379→            _display_send_results(results, title=f"Batch {batch_num} Send Results")
   380→
   381→            # Accumulate totals
   382→            for key in total_results:
   383→                total_results[key] += results[key]
   384→
   385→        # Final summary
   386→        console.print(f"\n[bold magenta]━━━ Pipeline Complete ━━━[/bold magenta]")
   387→        console.print(f"Total unique candidates found: {len(seen_ids)}")
   388→        if not dry_run and total_results['total'] > 0:
   389→            _display_send_results(total_results, title="Overall Send Results")
   390→
   391→    def _display_candidates_table(candidates, title):
   392→        """Display a table of candidates."""
   393→        table = Table(title=f"{title} ({len(candidates)} candidates)")
   394→        table.add_column("#", style="dim", justify="right")
   395→        table.add_column("Name", style="cyan")
   396→        table.add_column("Title", style="green")
   397→        table.add_column("Company", style="yellow")
   398→        table.add_column("ID", style="dim")
   399→
   400→        for i, c in enumerate(candidates[:50], 1):
   401→            table.add_row(str(i), c.name, c.title, c.company, c.maimai_id)
   402→
   403→        console.print(table)
   404→
   405→        if len(candidates) > 50:
   406→            console.print(f"[dim]... and {len(candidates) - 50} more[/dim]")
   407→
   408→    async def _send_batch(candidates, template, variables, browser, skip_messaged, interactive=False):
   409→        """Send messages to a batch of candidates."""
   410→        candidate_dicts = [
   411→            {
   412→                'maimai_id': c.maimai_id,
   413→                'name': c.name,
   414→                'company': c.company,
   415→                'title': c.title,
   416→            }
   417→            for c in candidates
   418→        ]
   419→
   420→        return await send_message_batch(
   421→            candidates=candidate_dicts,
   422→            template_name=template,
   423→            common_variables=variables,
   424→            browser=browser,
   425→            skip_messaged=skip_messaged,
   426→            interactive=interactive,
   427→        )
   428→
   429→    def _display_send_results(results, title="Send Results"):
   430→        """Display send results summary table."""
   431→        console.print()
   432→        result_table = Table(title=title)
   433→        result_table.add_column("Metric", style="bold")
   434→        result_table.add_column("Count", justify="right")
   435→
   436→        result_table.add_row("Total candidates", str(results['total']))
   437→        result_table.add_row("Sent", f"[green]{results['sent']}[/green]")
   438→        result_table.add_row("Failed", f"[red]{results['failed']}[/red]")
   439→        result_table.add_row("Skipped (already messaged)", f"[yellow]{results['skipped']}[/yellow]")
   440→        result_table.add_row("Rate limited", f"[yellow]{results['rate_limited']}[/yellow]")
   441→
   442→        console.print(result_table)
   443→
   444→    run_async(_pipeline())

1→"""Random delay generators for human-like behavior simulation."""
     2→
     3→import random
     4→import asyncio
     5→from typing import Tuple
     6→
     7→from src.utils.config import get_config
     8→
     9→
    10→def get_random_delay(delay_type: str = 'action') -> float:
    11→    """
    12→    Get a random delay based on configured ranges.
    13→
    14→    Args:
    15→        delay_type: Type of delay - 'page_load', 'action', 'typing', 'scroll'
    16→
    17→    Returns:
    18→        Random delay in seconds
    19→    """
    20→    config = get_config()
    21→
    22→    delay_ranges = {
    23→        'page_load': (
    24→            config.get('delays.page_load_min', 2),
    25→            config.get('delays.page_load_max', 5)
    26→        ),
    27→        'action': (
    28→            config.get('delays.action_min', 0.5),
    29→            config.get('delays.action_max', 2.0)
    30→        ),
    31→        'typing': (
    32→            config.get('delays.typing_min', 0.05),
    33→            config.get('delays.typing_max', 0.15)
    34→        ),
    35→        'scroll': (
    36→            config.get('delays.scroll_min', 0.5),
    37→            config.get('delays.scroll_max', 1.5)
    38→        ),
    39→    }
    40→
    41→    min_delay, max_delay = delay_ranges.get(delay_type, (0.5, 2.0))
    42→    return random.uniform(min_delay, max_delay)
    43→
    44→
    45→async def async_random_delay(delay_type: str = 'action'):
    46→    """
    47→    Asynchronous version of get_random_delay.
    48→
    49→    Args:
    50→        delay_type: Type of delay - 'page_load', 'action', 'typing', 'scroll'
    51→    """
    52→    delay = get_random_delay(delay_type)
    53→    await asyncio.sleep(delay)
    54→
    55→
    56→def sync_random_delay(delay_type: str = 'action'):
    57→    """
    58→    Synchronous sleep with random delay.
    59→
    60→    Args:
    61→        delay_type: Type of delay - 'page_load', 'action', 'typing', 'scroll'
    62→    """
    63→    import time
    64→    delay = get_random_delay(delay_type)
    65→    time.sleep(delay)
    66→
    67→
    68→async def random_scroll_delay() -> None:
    69→    """Random delay suitable for scrolling actions."""
    70→    await async_random_delay('scroll')
    71→
    72→
    73→async def random_page_load_delay() -> None:
    74→    """Random delay suitable for page load waits."""
    75→    await async_random_delay('page_load')
    76→
    77→
    78→def get_retry_delay(attempt: int, base_delay: float = 1.0, max_delay: float = 60.0) -> float:
    79→    """
    80→    Calculate exponential backoff delay for retries.
    81→
    82→    Args:
    83→        attempt: Current retry attempt number (0-indexed)
    84→        base_delay: Base delay in seconds
    85→        max_delay: Maximum delay cap
    86→
    87→    Returns:
    88→        Delay in seconds with jitter
    89→    """
    90→    # Exponential backoff: base * 2^attempt
    91→    delay = min(base_delay * (2 ** attempt), max_delay)
    92→
    93→    # Add jitter (±25%)
    94→    jitter = delay * random.uniform(-0.25, 0.25)
    95→    return max(0, delay + jitter)
    96→

     2→
     3→from tenacity import retry, stop_after_attempt, wait_exponential, retry_if_exception_type
     4→from playwright.async_api import Page, Error as PlaywrightError
     5→from loguru import logger
     6→
     7→
     8→@retry(
     9→    stop=stop_after_attempt(3),
    10→    wait=wait_exponential(multiplier=1, min=2, max=10),
    11→    retry=retry_if_exception_type((PlaywrightError, TimeoutError, ConnectionError)),
    12→    before_sleep=lambda retry_state: logger.warning(
    13→        f"Navigation failed (attempt {retry_state.attempt_number}), retrying..."
    14→    ),
    15→    reraise=True,
    16→)
    17→async def retry_navigation(page: Page, url: str, **kwargs) -> None:
    18→    """Navigate to a URL with automatic retry on failure.
    19→
    20→    Args:
    21→        page: Playwright page instance
    22→        url: URL to navigate to
    23→        **kwargs: Additional arguments passed to page.goto()
    24→    """
    25→    await page.goto(url, **kwargs)
    26→

1→"""Session management for persistent browser sessions.
     2→
     3→Handles saving and loading browser sessions (cookies, localStorage)
     4→to enable resuming without re-login.
     5→"""
     6→
     7→import json
     8→from pathlib import Path
     9→from datetime import datetime, timedelta
    10→from typing import Optional
    11→
    12→from playwright.async_api import Page, BrowserContext
    13→from loguru import logger
    14→
    15→from src.utils.config import get_config
    16→from src.exceptions.errors import SessionExpiredError
    17→
    18→
    19→class SessionManager:
    20→    """Manages browser session persistence."""
    21→
    22→    def __init__(self, session_path: Optional[str] = None):
    23→        """
    24→        Initialize session manager.
    25→
    26→        Args:
    27→            session_path: Path to session file. Uses config default if None.
    28→        """
    29→        if session_path is None:
    30→            config = get_config()
    31→            session_path = config.get('data.session_path', 'data/.session/maimai_session.json')
    32→
    33→        self.session_path = Path(session_path)
    34→        self._ensure_session_dir()
    35→
    36→    def _ensure_session_dir(self) -> None:
    37→        """Ensure session directory exists."""
    38→        self.session_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    39→
    40→    def has_saved_session(self) -> bool:
    41→        """
    42→        Check if a saved session exists.
    43→
    44→        Returns:
    45→            True if session file exists
    46→        """
    47→        return self.session_path.exists()
    48→
    49→    async def save(self, context: BrowserContext) -> None:
    50→        """
    51→        Save browser session state.
    52→
    53→        Args:
    54→            context: Playwright browser context
    55→        """
    56→        try:
    57→            # Get storage state (cookies + localStorage)
    58→            storage_state = await context.storage_state()
    59→
    60→            # Add metadata
    61→            session_data = {
    62→                'saved_at': datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
    63→                'storage_state': storage_state
    64→            }
    65→
    66→            # Write to file
    67→            with open(self.session_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    68→                json.dump(session_data, f, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
    69→
    70→            logger.info(f"Session saved to {self.session_path}")
    71→
    72→        except Exception as e:
    73→            logger.error(f"Failed to save session: {e}")
    74→            raise
    75→
    76→    async def load(self, context: BrowserContext) -> bool:
    77→        """
    78→        Load saved session into browser context.
    79→
    80→        Args:
    81→            context: Playwright browser context
    82→
    83→        Returns:
    84→            True if session was loaded successfully
    85→        """
    86→        if not self.has_saved_session():
    87→            logger.debug("No saved session found")
    88→            return False
    89→
    90→        try:
    91→            with open(self.session_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    92→                session_data = json.load(f)
    93→
    94→            # Check session age (warn if old)
    95→            saved_at = datetime.fromisoformat(session_data.get('saved_at', '2000-01-01'))
    96→            age = datetime.utcnow() - saved_at
    97→
    98→            if age > timedelta(days=7):
    99→                logger.warning(f"Session is {age.days} days old, may be expired")
   100→
   101→            # Apply storage state
   102→            storage_state = session_data.get('storage_state', {})
   103→
   104→            # Add cookies
   105→            cookies = storage_state.get('cookies', [])
   106→            if cookies:
   107→                await context.add_cookies(cookies)
   108→                logger.debug(f"Loaded {len(cookies)} cookies")
   109→
   110→            logger.info("Session loaded successfully")
   111→            return True
   112→
   113→        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:
   114→            logger.error(f"Invalid session file: {e}")
   115→            return False
   116→        except Exception as e:
   117→            logger.error(f"Failed to load session: {e}")
   118→            return False
   119→
   120→    def get_storage_state_path(self) -> Optional[str]:
   121→        """
   122→        Get path to storage state file if it exists.
   123→
   124→        Used for initializing browser context with saved state.
   125→
   126→        Returns:
   127→            Path string if exists, None otherwise
   128→        """
   129→        if not self.has_saved_session():
   130→            return None
   131→
   132→        try:
   133→            with open(self.session_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
   134→                session_data = json.load(f)
   135→
   136→            # Extract just the storage state and save to temp file
   137→            storage_state = session_data.get('storage_state', {})
   138→
   139→            # Write storage state to a separate file for Playwright
   140→            state_path = self.session_path.parent / 'storage_state.json'
   141→            with open(state_path, 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
   142→                json.dump(storage_state, f)
   143→
   144→            return str(state_path)
   145→
   146→        except Exception as e:
   147→            logger.error(f"Failed to get storage state path: {e}")
   148→            return None
   149→
   150→    async def is_valid(self, page: Page) -> bool:
   151→        """
   152→        Check if current session is valid (user is logged in).
   153→
   154→        Detects login state by checking for logged-in indicators on page.
   155→
   156→        Args:
   157→            page: Playwright page instance
   158→
   159→        Returns:
   160→            True if session appears valid
   161→        """
   162→        try:
   163→            config = get_config()
   164→
   165→            # Try to find logged-in indicator elements
   166→            logged_in_selector = config.get_selector('login.logged_in_indicator')
   167→
   168→            # Wait briefly for indicator
   169→            try:
   170→                await page.wait_for_selector(
   171→                    logged_in_selector,
   172→                    timeout=5000,
   173→                    state='visible'
   174→                )
   175→                logger.debug("Session is valid - logged in indicator found")
   176→                return True
   177→            except Exception:
   178→                logger.debug("Session invalid - logged in indicator not found")
   179→                return False
   180→
   181→        except Exception as e:
   182→            logger.error(f"Error checking session validity: {e}")
   183→            return False
   184→
   185→    async def clear(self) -> None:
   186→        """Clear saved session."""
   187→        if self.session_path.exists():
   188→            self.session_path.unlink()
   189→            logger.info("Session cleared")
   190→
   191→        # Also clear storage state file if exists
   192→        state_path = self.session_path.parent / 'storage_state.json'
   193→        if state_path.exists():
   194→            state_path.unlink()
   195→
   196→    def get_session_info(self) -> Optional[dict]:
   197→        """
   198→        Get information about saved session.
   199→
   200→        Returns:
   201→            Dict with session metadata or None if no session
   202→        """
   203→        if not self.has_saved_session():
   204→            return None
   205→
   206→        try:
   207→            with open(self.session_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
   208→                session_data = json.load(f)
   209→
   210→            saved_at = session_data.get('saved_at')
   211→            storage_state = session_data.get('storage_state', {})
   212→            cookies = storage_state.get('cookies', [])
   213→
   214→            # Calculate age
   215→            if saved_at:
   216→                saved_datetime = datetime.fromisoformat(saved_at)
   217→                age = datetime.utcnow() - saved_datetime
   218→                age_str = f"{age.days}d {age.seconds // 3600}h"
   219→            else:
   220→                age_str = "unknown"
   221→
   222→            return {
   223→                'saved_at': saved_at,
   224→                'age': age_str,
   225→                'cookie_count': len(cookies),
   226→            }
   227→
   228→        except Exception as e:
   229→            logger.error(f"Failed to get session info: {e}")
   230→            return None
   231→
   232→
   233→# Global session manager instance
   234→_session_manager = None
   235→
   236→
   237→def get_session_manager() -> SessionManager:
   238→    """Get global session manager instance."""
   239→    global _session_manager
   240→    if _session_manager is None:
   241→        _session_manager = SessionManager()
   242→    return _session_manager
   243→

1→"""Authentication module for Maimai login.
     2→
     3→Handles manual login flow with session persistence.
     4→The user logs in manually in the browser window,
     5→and the session is saved for future automated operations.
     6→"""
     7→
     8→import asyncio
     9→from typing import Optional
    10→
    11→from playwright.async_api import Page
    12→from loguru import logger
    13→from rich.console import Console
    14→from rich.panel import Panel
    15→
    16→from src.core.browser import BrowserManager, get_browser_manager
    17→from src.core.session import get_session_manager
    18→from src.utils.config import get_config
    19→from src.exceptions.errors import BrowserError, SessionExpiredError
    20→
    21→console = Console()
    22→
    23→
    24→async def _check_captcha(page: Page) -> None:
    25→    """Detect captcha elements on page and pause for manual resolution."""
    26→    captcha_selectors = [
    27→        '[class*="captcha"]',
    28→        '[class*="verify"]',
    29→        '[id*="captcha"]',
    30→        '[class*="slider"]',
    31→        '[class*="geetest"]',
    32→        'iframe[src*="captcha"]',
    33→    ]
    34→    for selector in captcha_selectors:
    35→        try:
    36→            elem = await page.query_selector(selector)
    37→            if elem and await elem.is_visible():
    38→                console.print("[bold red]Captcha detected! Please solve it in the browser window.[/bold red]")
    39→                console.print("[dim]Press Enter here after solving the captcha...[/dim]")
    40→                import sys
    41→                sys.stdin.readline()
    42→                await page.wait_for_timeout(2000)
    43→                return
    44→        except Exception:
    45→            continue
    46→
    47→
    48→# Maimai URLs
    49→MAIMAI_HOME = "https://maimai.cn"
    50→MAIMAI_LOGIN = "https://maimai.cn/login"
    51→
    52→
    53→async def login(browser: Optional[BrowserManager] = None) -> bool:
    54→    """
    55→    Perform manual login to Maimai.
    56→
    57→    Opens browser for user to manually log in (scan QR code or enter credentials).
    58→    Saves session after successful login for future use.
    59→
    60→    Args:
    61→        browser: Browser manager instance (uses global if None)
    62→
    63→    Returns:
    64→        True if login successful
    65→    """
    66→    if browser is None:
    67→        browser = get_browser_manager()
    68→
    69→    config = get_config()
    70→    session_manager = get_session_manager()
    71→
    72→    # Check for existing valid session
    73→    if session_manager.has_saved_session():
    74→        console.print("[cyan]Found saved session, checking validity...[/cyan]")
    75→
    76→        page = await browser.start(headless=False)
    77→
    78→        # check_login_status navigates to a protected page internally
    79→        # to trigger server-side auth verification
    80→        if await check_login_status(page):
    81→            console.print("[green]Session is valid, already logged in![/green]")
    82→            return True
    83→        else:
    84→            console.print("[yellow]Saved session expired, need to re-login[/yellow]")
    85→            # Clear the invalid session so it won't be loaded again
    86→            await session_manager.clear()
    87→            logger.info("Cleared expired session")
    88→            # Restart browser to get a clean context without stale cookies
    89→            await browser.close()
    90→            page = await browser.start(headless=False)
    91→
    92→    # Start browser for manual login
    93→    console.print(Panel(
    94→        "[bold]Manual Login Required[/bold]\n\n"
    95→        "A browser window will open. Please log in manually:\n"
    96→        "1. Scan QR code with Maimai app, OR\n"
    97→        "2. Enter phone number and verification code\n\n"
    98→        "[dim]The window will close automatically after login.[/dim]",
    99→        title="Maimai Login",
   100→        border_style="cyan"
   101→    ))
   102→
   103→    try:
   104→        page = await browser.start(headless=False)
   105→
   106→        # Navigate to login page
   107→        await page.goto(MAIMAI_LOGIN, wait_until='domcontentloaded')
   108→        logger.info("Opened login page, waiting for user to log in...")
   109→
   110→        # Poll for login success
   111→        max_wait_seconds = 300  # 5 minutes timeout
   112→        poll_interval = 2  # Check every 2 seconds
   113→        elapsed = 0
   114→
   115→        console.print("[dim]Waiting for login... (timeout: 5 minutes)[/dim]")
   116→
   117→        while elapsed < max_wait_seconds:
   118→            await asyncio.sleep(poll_interval)
   119→            elapsed += poll_interval
   120→
   121→            # Check for captcha during login
   122→            await _check_captcha(page)
   123→
   124→            current_url = page.url
   125→
   126→            # Check if URL changed from login page (potential login success)
   127→            if 'login' not in current_url.lower() and 'maimai.cn' in current_url:
   128→                logger.info(f"Login page redirected to: {current_url}")
   129→                # Wait for page to fully load after redirect
   130→                await page.wait_for_timeout(3000)
   131→
   132→                # Verify by navigating to a protected page (triggers real auth check)
   133→                if await check_login_status(page):
   134→                    console.print("[green]Login successful![/green]")
   135→                    await browser.save_session()
   136→                    console.print("[green]Session saved for future use[/green]")
   137→                    return True
   138→                else:
   139→                    # Redirected but not actually logged in, navigate back to login
   140→                    logger.warning("Redirected from login but not authenticated, retrying...")
   141→                    await page.goto(MAIMAI_LOGIN, wait_until='domcontentloaded')
   142→                    continue
   143→
   144→        # Timeout
   145→        console.print("[red]Login timeout. Please try again.[/red]")
   146→        return False
   147→
   148→    except Exception as e:
   149→        logger.error(f"Login error: {e}")
   150→        console.print(f"[red]Login error: {e}[/red]")
   151→        return False
   152→
   153→
   154→async def check_login_status(page: Page) -> bool:
   155→    """
   156→    Check if user is currently logged in by navigating to a protected page
   157→    and observing whether Maimai redirects to the login page.
   158→
   159→    API probes can give false positives (server returns 200 but session is
   160→    actually stale). The redirect-based approach is reliable because Maimai's
   161→    server-side auth check only fires on real page navigations, and it will
   162→    redirect to the login page if the session is invalid.
   163→
   164→    Args:
   165→        page: Playwright page instance
   166→
   167→    Returns:
   168→        True if logged in (no redirect to login page)
   169→    """
   170→    try:
   171→        # Navigate to a protected page that requires authentication.
   172→        # The search center page reliably triggers server-side auth checks.
   173→        protected_url = "https://maimai.cn/web/search_center"
   174→        current_url = page.url
   175→
   176→        # Only navigate if we're not already on a protected maimai page
   177→        if 'maimai.cn' not in current_url or 'login' in current_url:
   178→            await page.goto(protected_url, wait_until='domcontentloaded')
   179→        else:
   180→            # We're on maimai but need to trigger an auth check.
   181→            # Navigate to the protected page to force the server-side check.
   182→            await page.goto(protected_url, wait_until='domcontentloaded')
   183→
   184→        # Wait for any redirects to settle (server-side auth redirect happens
   185→        # after initial page load and JS execution)
   186→        await page.wait_for_timeout(3000)
   187→
   188→        # Check 1: URL-based detection - did we get redirected to login?
   189→        final_url = page.url
   190→        if 'login' in final_url.lower():
   191→            logger.info(f"Login status check: logged_in=False, detail=redirected_to_login (url={final_url})")
   192→            return False
   193→
   194→        # Check 2: Page content detection - look for login form elements
   195→        # even if URL didn't change (some login overlays don't change the URL)
   196→        is_login_page = await page.evaluate("""
   197→            () => {
   198→                const bodyText = document.body ? document.body.innerText : '';
   199→                const loginIndicators = [
   200→                    '欢迎使用脉脉',
   201→                    '登录/注册',
   202→                    '请输入手机号',
   203→                    '获取验证码',
   204→                    '扫码登录',
   205→                    '手机号登录'
   206→                ];
   207→                for (const indicator of loginIndicators) {
   208→                    if (bodyText.includes(indicator)) {
   209→                        return { is_login: true, matched: indicator };
   210→                    }
   211→                }
   212→
   213→                // Also check for login form elements in DOM
   214→                const loginForm = document.querySelector(
   215→                    'input[type="tel"], input[placeholder*="手机"], ' +
   216→                    '[class*="qrcode"], [class*="scan-login"]'
   217→                );
   218→                if (loginForm) {
   219→                    return { is_login: true, matched: 'login_form_element' };
   220→                }
   221→
   222→                return { is_login: false, matched: null };
   223→            }
   224→        """)
   225→
   226→        if is_login_page.get('is_login', False):
   227→            detail = is_login_page.get('matched', 'unknown')
   228→            logger.info(f"Login status check: logged_in=False, detail=login_page_detected ({detail})")
   229→            return False
   230→
   231→        logger.info(f"Login status check: logged_in=True, detail=protected_page_accessible (url={final_url})")
   232→        return True
   233→
   234→    except Exception as e:
   235→        logger.error(f"Error checking login status: {e}")
   236→        return False
   237→
   238→
   239→async def ensure_logged_in(browser: Optional[BrowserManager] = None) -> bool:
   240→    """
   241→    Ensure user is logged in, prompting for login if needed.
   242→
   243→    Args:
   244→        browser: Browser manager instance
   245→
   246→    Returns:
   247→        True if logged in (existing or new login)
   248→
   249→    Raises:
   250→        SessionExpiredError: If login fails
   251→    """
   252→    if browser is None:
   253→        browser = get_browser_manager()
   254→
   255→    # Start browser if not running (need visible browser for manual login)
   256→    if not browser.is_running:
   257→        await browser.start(headless=False)
   258→
   259→    # check_login_status navigates to a protected page internally,
   260→    # so no need to pre-navigate to the homepage
   261→    if browser.page:
   262→        if await check_login_status(browser.page):
   263→            return True
   264→
   265→    # Need to login
   266→    console.print("[yellow]Not logged in. Starting login process...[/yellow]")
   267→
   268→    if await login(browser):
   269→        return True
   270→
   271→    raise SessionExpiredError("Failed to log in to Maimai")
   272→
   273→
   274→async def logout(browser: Optional[BrowserManager] = None) -> None:
   275→    """
   276→    Log out and clear saved session.
   277→
   278→    Args:
   279→        browser: Browser manager instance
   280→    """
   281→    if browser is None:
   282→        browser = get_browser_manager()
   283→
   284→    session_manager = get_session_manager()
   285→
   286→    # Clear saved session
   287→    await session_manager.clear()
   288→
   289→    # Close browser
   290→    await browser.close()
   291→
   292→    console.print("[green]Logged out and session cleared[/green]")
   293→

1→"""Interactive selector discovery for chat pages.
     2→
     3→When automatic selectors fail, this module pauses execution and lets
     4→the user manually navigate to the correct chat page in the browser.
     5→It then inspects the DOM to discover working selectors, which are
     6→reused for all remaining candidates in the session.
     7→"""
     8→
     9→import asyncio
    10→from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    11→from typing import Optional
    12→
    13→from playwright.async_api import Page
    14→from loguru import logger
    15→from rich.console import Console
    16→from rich.panel import Panel
    17→from rich.text import Text
    18→
    19→from src.core.anti_detection import human_type, human_click
    20→from src.utils.delays import async_random_delay
    21→
    22→console = Console()
    23→
    24→# JavaScript to scan DOM for interactive elements
    25→DISCOVER_ELEMENTS_JS = """
    26→() => {
    27→    const results = [];
    28→
    29→    // Find visible buttons
    30→    const buttons = document.querySelectorAll('button, [role="button"], a[class*="btn"], div[class*="btn"]');
    31→    buttons.forEach((el, i) => {
    32→        const rect = el.getBoundingClientRect();
    33→        if (rect.width > 0 && rect.height > 0) {
    34→            const text = (el.textContent || '').trim().substring(0, 50);
    35→            const tag = el.tagName.toLowerCase();
    36→            const classes = el.className ? String(el.className).substring(0, 80) : '';
    37→            const id = el.id || '';
    38→            results.push({
    39→                type: 'button',
    40→                index: results.length,
    41→                tag: tag,
    42→                text: text,
    43→                classes: classes,
    44→                id: id,
    45→                selector: id ? `#${id}` : (classes ? `${tag}.${String(el.className).split(' ')[0]}` : `${tag}:nth-of-type(${i+1})`),
    46→            });
    47→        }
    48→    });
    49→
    50→    // Find visible textareas and contenteditable elements
    51→    const inputs = document.querySelectorAll('textarea, [contenteditable="true"], input[type="text"]');
    52→    inputs.forEach((el, i) => {
    53→        const rect = el.getBoundingClientRect();
    54→        if (rect.width > 0 && rect.height > 0) {
    55→            const tag = el.tagName.toLowerCase();
    56→            const classes = el.className ? String(el.className).substring(0, 80) : '';
    57→            const id = el.id || '';
    58→            const placeholder = el.getAttribute('placeholder') || '';
    59→            const contenteditable = el.getAttribute('contenteditable') || '';
    60→            results.push({
    61→                type: 'input',
    62→                index: results.length,
    63→                tag: tag,
    64→                text: placeholder,
    65→                classes: classes,
    66→                id: id,
    67→                contenteditable: contenteditable,
    68→                selector: id ? `#${id}` : (classes ? `${tag}.${String(el.className).split(' ')[0]}` : (contenteditable ? '[contenteditable="true"]' : `${tag}:nth-of-type(${i+1})`)),
    69→            });
    70→        }
    71→    });
    72→
    73→    return results;
    74→}
    75→"""
    76→
    77→MAX_DISCOVERY_RETRIES = 3
    78→
    79→
    80→@dataclass
    81→class DiscoveredSelectors:
    82→    """Session-scoped container for discovered chat page selectors."""
    83→
    84→    chat_button: Optional[str] = None
    85→    message_input: Optional[str] = None
    86→    send_button: Optional[str] = None
    87→    chat_url_pattern: Optional[str] = None
    88→    skip_chat_button: bool = False
    89→    is_populated: bool = False
